current_chart_value: Julianne Ficzeri, a 68-year-old female, is being admitted to home health care following her recent right total knee arthroplasty performed on March 12, 2026. Her primary diagnosis is right knee osteoarthritis (ICD10CM - M17.11), and she has a secondary condition of chronic pain related to the pre-operative osteoarthritis. Due to the surgical intervention, she is medically homebound, as leaving the home requires significant effort and assistance, which has been documented in her hospital and clinical visit notes. At

current_chart_value: Julianne Ficzeri is medically homebound due to her recent right total knee arthroplasty. She requires significant effort and assistance to leave her home, as documented in her hospital and clinical visit notes. Her condition necessitates intermittent skilled nursing care and physical therapy to support her recovery and rehabilitation process.

current_chart_value: Julianne Ficzeri reports post-surgical knee pain with a pain intensity of 7 on the Wong-Baker FACES® Pain Rating Scale. The pain is described as a dull ache and is exacerbated by movement. The pain affects her ability to sleep and participate in day-to-day activities.
